message:
feat(net,io): add Cx cancellation checkpoint to all async I/O poll methods

Wire cooperative cancellation into every async read, write, flush, and
shutdown path across TCP (borrowed and owned split halves), UDP, Unix
(stream, datagram, borrowed and owned splits), WebSocket (client, server,
split), and the io::copy bridge. Each poll_* method now checks
`Cx::current().is_some_and(|cx| cx.checkpoint().is_err())` at entry and
returns `io::ErrorKind::Interrupted` when the owning scope has requested
cancellation, ensuring that long-running I/O loops honour structured
cancellation without requiring explicit token plumbing.

Includes regression tests for borrowed TCP split halves and WebSocket
cancel behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
